channel.title: Neeraj's Tech Lab
channel.description: Recent content on Neeraj's Tech Lab

item.title: ARM RD-N2 SoC Architecture — A Friendly Deep Dive
item.description: A comprehensive deep-dive into the ARM RD-N2 reference design SoC — covering the SCP, CMN-700 mesh, bus hierarchy, MHU, SCMI, power domains, PLL, and DVFS — explained like a friend over coffee.

item.title: Booting Linux on the ARM RD-N2 FVP — A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: A friendly step-by-step guide to booting Linux on the ARM Neoverse RD-N2 Fixed Virtual Platform — from installation through firmware build to successful boot.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hi, I’m Neeraj 👋

I’m a systems engineer fascinated by what happens before the OS boots — the firmware, the silicon, the power sequencing, the interconnects, and all the invisible choreography that makes modern processors work.

This blog is my lab notebook. I write about:

  • ARM Architecture — Neoverse, Cortex-M, SoC design
  • Firmware & Boot — SCP, TF-A, UEFI, secure boot chains
  • Silicon Internals — Interconnects (CMN-700), power domains, PLLs, DVFS
  • Hands-on Experiments — Building and booting on FVPs, QEMU, real hardware

Everything here is written in a conversational tone because I believe complex topics deserve clear explanations, not jargon walls.
